etymology.description: 巴(bā, "to hold onto; cling") 掌(zhǎng, "palm; to slap")
etymology.interpretation: 巴(bā, "to hold onto; cling to") suggests grasping onto something, which links to the idea of a 'palm' where one holds or grasps items. 掌(zhǎng, "palm; to slap") directly describes the flat, broad shape of the hand's palm, fitting its role in slapping. Together, 巴掌(bāzhang) conveys the sense of a palm, a part of the hand capable of clinging and slapping.

examples.chinese: 他愤怒地拍了一下巴掌(他憤怒地拍了一下巴掌)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fènnù de pāile yīxià bāzhang.

examples.chinese: 孩子用力地击了一下自己的巴掌(孩子用力地擊了一下自己的巴掌)。
examples.pinyin: Háizi yònglì de jīle yīxià zìjǐ de bāzhang.
examples.english: The child forcefully slapped his own palm.

examples.chinese: 他的巴掌打得很响(他的巴掌打得很響)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de bāzhang dǎ de hěn xiǎng.
